context: Evaluates whether the candidate correctly implements a CVE impact analysis workflow using the rh-sre cve-impact skill pattern. The workflow must validate MCP prerequisites, retrieve vulnerability data with correct parameters, identify and classify affected systems, and produce a structured risk assessment.

checklist.name: MCP prerequisite validation step
checklist.description: The workflow begins with a step that invokes a prerequisite validator sub-skill before any CVE query. The step handles all three outcomes: PASSED (continue), PARTIAL (warn user and offer to proceed), FAILED (stop and provide setup instructions).

checklist.name: CVE ID format in parameters
checklist.description: The CVE detail retrieval step specifies the cve_id parameter in the exact format 'CVE-YYYY-NNNNN' (e.g., 'CVE-2024-1234'). The parameter includes_details is set to true to get full metadata.

checklist.name: Correct system identifier parameter
checklist.description: The affected systems retrieval step uses 'system_uuid' (not 'system_id' or 'id') when calling the system-level CVE tool, and uses include_patched: false to focus on vulnerable systems.

checklist.name: System classification step
checklist.description: After identifying affected systems, the workflow includes a classification step that categorizes systems by environment (production/staging/development) and by type (bare metal/virtual machine).

checklist.name: Risk assessment output structure
checklist.description: The risk assessment output includes: Overall Risk Level (Critical/High/Medium/Low), a list of contributing factors (CVSS score, affected systems count, environment, attack vector, exploitability), a recommendation, and a priority tier (P0/P1/P2) with time guidance.

checklist.name: Document consultation for CVSS
checklist.description: The step that performs CVSS interpretation includes a Document Consultation block that reads the CVSS scoring reference document before making risk determinations.
